title: Events, Held State, Actions, Remapping, Focus, and Devices · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for events, held state, actions, remapping, focus, and devices and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, pygame-ce, SDL, display, input, audio, filesystem, packaging, GPU, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for device-independent remappable action layer. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal deb…
objectives: Drain or pump the bounded event queue every frame and distinguish ordered events from sampled device state.
Map press, release, repeat, held, analog, and one-shot behavior to named actions with testable transitions.
Store configurable action bindings, detect conflicts, offer alternatives, and avoid unmodified character-key shortcuts where inappropriate.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Event Queue · changed case · Quiz Event Queue
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 1871 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Drain or pump the bounded event queue every frame and distinguish ordered events from sampled device state.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Event Queue
steps.instruction:
Order the Event Queue Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python player-input-to-release evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Event Queue.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Event Queue assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Event Queue, keep Event Queue connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.text: A quiz Asteroids team handles case 4536 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Drain or pump the bounded event queue every frame and distinguish ordered events from sampled device state.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Event Queue has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Event Queue, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Polling key state eliminates the need to process events.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Input Accessibility
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Input Accessibility: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 9393 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Support keyboard and controller, reduced-complexity modes, toggle or hold options, sensitivity, dead zones, timing relief, and pause.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Input Accessibility.
steps.why: Retrieving Input Accessibility through the concrete device-independent remappable action layer case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Input Accessibility, keep Input Accessibility connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Support keyboard and controller, reduced-complexity modes, toggle or hold options, sensitivity, dead zones, timing relief, and pause.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Support keyboard and controller, reduced-complexity modes, toggle or hold options, sensitivity, dead zones, timing relief, and pause.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Input Accessibility, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Adding controller support automatically makes input accessible.
